title: TB-500 (Thymosin Beta-4): The Recovery Peptide Explained
deck: A fragment of thymosin beta-4 that promotes cell migration and actin polymerization, popular in sports recovery circles with preclinical wound-healing data and limited human trials.
metaDescription: TB-500 peptide explained: thymosin beta-4 mechanism, 2.5mg protocols, tissue repair preclinical data, WADA ban, and evidence gaps.
bodyHtml:
TB-500 is a synthetic peptide corresponding to a region of thymosin beta-4, a 43-amino-acid protein involved in actin sequestration, cell migration, and angiogenesis. It is used in compounded injectable protocols (often 2.5 mg twice weekly) for musculoskeletal recovery, often stacked with BPC-157.

Preclinical models show accelerated wound closure and tendon repair. Human RCT evidence for athletic injury is sparse. TB-500 is prohibited in sport by WADA. Compounded product requires licensed pharmacy sourcing.

CLYR Health offers TB-500 as a peptides preview SKU for off-label recovery protocols under prescriber supervision.

sources.label: Goldstein AL, et al. Thymosin beta-4: actin-sequestering protein. Ann NY Acad Sci. 2007.
Philp D, et al. Thymosin beta-4 and wound healing. Ann NY Acad Sci. 2012.
